transcript: All right. So I have been tasked with giving a product keynote. I'm a venture capitalist. My product... it's giving people money. Once they have the money, helping them, raising some more money to give people more money. Uh, so the product is going to be a little different than some of the keynotes that you've heard here over the past couple of days. Uh, I run 6th Man Ventures. We're an early stage crypto VC firm. Uh, it means, yeah, many things to many different people. To us, it means we invest typically pre-launch, so at the pre-seed stage. At the seed stage, a product that maybe has just been launched, it's on testnet. Um, or Series A, so something that's been launched, it's on mainnet, and it's starting to grow. Uh, and we invest at the crypto application layer. So Solana is a natural home to us. It's the place where, you know, the highest concentration of revenue producing apps, of users, of fees, uh, you name it. So we spend approximately 50% of our time investing in Solana. We are ourselves a group of founders and builders. And I think over the next couple years, you're going to see that become really important as we move from sort of the protocol layer up to the application layer, where the people who are building need experienced partners next to them who have built businesses themselves. So I've built businesses. I've also helped launch things like BONK, you know, crypto builders are unique, we're different, we're, you know, kind of quirky. Uh, LinksDAO. And I've built traditional businesses in Web2, as has our team. Our goal and what we measure ourselves on is how many folks are we helping to introduce to crypto. So the companies that we back have introduced and served tens of millions of users in the aggregate, uh, and have created billions in enterprise value. A lot of that, by the way, flowing from real fee generation, not from, you know, sort of inflated exchange trading metrics and things like that. Uh, we're partners to founders. So VC, it's critical not to be like that traditional suited guy on Sand Hill Road. You know, it's it's sort of the guy wearing like the... I'm kind of a boomer, but I like to get in the trenches with the folks that we work with. And we do. We became VCs, we became investors because we'd been builders, uh, and we wanted to work with more people building, uh, and we were using so many different things from, you know, all the things that haven't worked to things that did work. And we just love doing things on-chain. Last thing, we've seen a trend of people over-raising. So raising tens of millions of dollars, hundreds of millions of dollars. There's an abundance of capital in crypto. You've seen it in the public markets with so many billions going to DAOs. Uh, we try to be very, very bespoke, very focused, do something like one deal per month. We like to lead or co-lead rounds, meaning we can get in there with the folks that we're backing. Many of them in this room and many of them on Solana. So these are some of the companies and some of the themes that we invest in. The reason I show you this is not to pitch our thesis. The gentleman before me was, you know, brilliant. I'm I'm not quite that smart. We... we are sort of thematically driven. So we have broad areas that we like to invest in, and then we opportunistically look for the best, the finest teams building in those spaces. These are some of the ones that we've worked with in the past. Um, this is an old slide. It's about a year old, but it's relevant. Why do we invest so heavily in Solana? Solana's rate of growth, and this is still the case, but it was wildly the case in 2023 and 2024, as an ecosystem, as a group of users... you can see it here with how many people have been attending over the past three days. It's just growing faster than any other crypto ecosystem. And so it just makes sense to focus our time there. You know, we'll invest in the Hyperliquid ecosystem, we'll invest in the Base ecosystem, but frankly, you want to be where the users, where the developers, where the technologists are, and now increasingly where the institutions are. And I've mentioned a few times value accrual. I've stolen everybody, all the other VCs in the space's slides here, uh, and data from DeFiLlama. But what's so exciting is those orange ones are actually crypto companies. Crypto companies and protocols and projects are growing faster to revenue, to a hundred million in revenue, than traditional startups these days. So all those orange ones... uh, and most of these or many of them are on Solana. Pump, Jupiter, Axiom, uh, are all Solana focused. So it's just a really exciting time to be investing, to have our product, to be able to give people money, uh, to be able to help give them advice and help them grow. Uh, the last thing I'd like to show you, and you know, I just... I put this slide up there so it gets on the recording and we can look at it later. But basically for the last four years, we've hit on most of the major trends in a pretty big way as a firm. And we think those trends will continue. The app layer is where we believe the majority of crypto value will accrue over the coming years. So for the next three years, we plan to do the same thing we've been doing for the past five years and we're thrilled. If you're building apps in the Solana ecosystem, if you're building apps in other ecosystems, please come talk to me. Thank you.

# Solana Breakpoint 2025: Key Announcements and Stories from Saturday
Day Three maintained the high expectations with announcements from Solflare, Solana Mobile, and Colosseum, among others.
[Pablo](https://solanafloor.com/author/pablo)
Copy link
* Published:
* Edited:
It was said that this year’s Breakpoint would be Solana’s biggest conference yet, and I’m sure none of the several thousand delegates that made their way to the Etihad Arena this week would disagree with that sentiment.
Saturday’s session featured a diverse set of updates highlighting Solana’s continued expansion across hardware, real-world assets, and institutional finance, as well as side events such as Predictify, hosted by[Kalshi](https://x.com/Kalshi?s=20).
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999766576091025438?s=20)> The last day of Breakpoint 2025 delivered a mix of hackathon results, product demonstrations, and strategic commitments that underscored the network’s growing role as a settlement layer for both digital and traditional assets.
Here’s what you missed if you’re not in Abu Dhabi:
## At Breakpoint
1. Colosseum has announced winners for its Cypherpunk hackathon
* *Unruggable, the Solana-native hardware wallet, is crowned grand champion.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999765402453467212?s=20)> 1. Uranium Digital demoed their product, which enables institutions to buy Uranium onchain for the first time.
* *Settlement is happening fully on Solana*
* *The entire crypto experience is abstracted*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999744679441826044?s=20)> 1. RockawayX launches 3 vaults on Solana.
* *Risk-curated RWA vault on Kamino*
* *Additional vaults on Exponent Finance and MidasRWA*
* *Partners with Figure, Onre, Solstice, and Huma as professional curators for these vaults.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999753859456204968?s=20)> 1. Solana Mobile is expanding beyond its own devices to additional hardware.
* *Working with FXTech, MediaTek, and Trustonic to integrate the Solana Mobile stack at the chipset level for other Android devices.*
* *MediaTek ships roughly 50% of all Android devices globally.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999757725505642967?s=20)> 1. Splyce Finance introduces $SFULC on Solana.
* *$SFULC is a yield-bearing token offering 10% APY powered by Fulcrum Lending*
* *Powered by real cash flows from senior, secured multifamily credit.*
* *$SFULC can be minted with stablecoins or bought directly on Raydium.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999761140931526825?s=20)> 1. VC Firm 6th Man Ventures announces that it is focused on Solan

# ![Logo_Purple_w bg.png](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/33b684d4-4115-45ef-849e-b207935b0b70?size=3)
# Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana
December 04, 2025 09:00 ET| Source:[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/FT%20Intermediate%CE%B4%20Inc%C2%A7)FollowFigure Technology Solutions, Inc.
Share
* * * * * * * * ![Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/38b97e43-d99f-4a08-9e38-3577453d7f24/rwa-consortium.png)[](#)
*New coalition advances Figure’s vision of moving capital markets to blockchain, expands real-world yield to Solana, and sets the standard for RWA collaboration across chains*
SAN FRANCISCO, Dec. 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=sVrR0nCGbyoSGqLpmp3W226XOcDwCfx8V3EteYCwLGxXEo0igM8ivJlwMreqY9enDApKlqpkq2h9LGXlpppDneFNwYs_wf55flepAb6tunwEmh-xksGmM2ldyKQcRF4H)(“Figure”)(Nasdaq: FIGR), the leading blockchain-native capital marketplace for the origination, funding, sale, and trading of on-chain loan products and tokenized real-world assets (RWAs), today announced the launch of an RWA consortium, a landmark coalition that will expand access to Figure's more than $1 billion in monthly on-chain loan originations, starting on Solana. The consortium represents a coordinated effort by leading crypto platforms to support and drive adoption of PRIME, the liquid staking token built on the[Hastra liquidity protocol](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=4qB14oW0HW3wV6G7eIeftmMVA8az3rMOK8PAa0Y2-BRXRv-AIPxXC8jou5QwqZswqZL167DOJEXxgNXklTx7X5zNuk4nN-6TXUv6pOrCjxitPRgZlNKhBddz_5Xu1T9Hu1XH4kRiKqkttkiJeicO8MN-CNu8gmEyyquNATcxaPNC56uSFgFkmxuV7i1s-QvGo4s2-XxOi1gshATrL0nmDL5c78zLpeU5MWvBvHd1TKM=), developed in partnership with Figure and the Provenance Blockchain Foundation. PRIME is powered by the Democratized Prime decentralized lending protocol on the Provenance Blockchain, which allows users to lend against pools of tokenized Figure RWA loans. Through this coalition, everyday DeFi users can now access institutional-grade yields previously reserved for banks and accredited investors.
The RWA Consortium builds on Figure’s vision to bring capital markets on-chain, a goal most recently reinforced during its IPO and the expansion of its RWA ecosystem across multiple blockchains. By extending beyond the Provenance Blockchain to integrate with Layer 1 networks like Solana, Figure is partnering with Hastra to leverage Chainlink[CCIP](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=AoAEgzHaPbSD8H81yfVKVDXA32VlG1HlMnUL10_xbh1thIeREQF8WzIHuEqW8sPg7s78VxMYQfWySLEd-xz4Tg==)to power the cross-chain interoperability needed for a connected, multi-chain finance ecosystem. With over $19 billion in on-chain loan originations to date and a[70% market share](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=_8YSDjYvrN99A8tUsqQH01Y3JxsV6a895BAreWpCDDEb82mue5e6XzzI_TsT7oYru-CPY3g5Xe0uEA769WrIxfR1KhHLCDiIcXYiXdT8nwu4hb9Amh8nUkhSFupuvO2d)in the RWA private credit, Figure is uniquely positioned to bridge traditional credit markets with DeFi.
This initiative follows the recent debut of Hastra, a liquidity protocol incubated by Figure and Provenance Blockchain that allows the yields generated in Democratized Prime to reach decentralized finance, with all Hastra yield products secured by the Chainlink data standard. Hastra acts as a

tags.impact_reason: A crypto VC firm highlighting their focus on Solana and their approach to funding is a solid update, but not ecosystem-shattering.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic basketball court as the semantic anchor because it directly references the company's name, '6th Man Ventures,' and provides a powerful metaphor for a strategic playing field. The 50% pivot is visualized by splitting the court's aesthetic exactly in half, with one side representing the generic VC space and the other glowing with Solana's brand colors. I included the text '50%' as a large, architectural element integrated into the court's floor to anchor the core statistic of the story diegetically.
visual_idea.description: A dramatic, low-angle view of a futuristic basketball court made of polished, dark concrete. The court is split exactly in half by a glowing neon half-court line. On the left side, the court lines glow a sterile, corporate white. On the right side, all the lines, including the three-point arc and key, pulse with Solana's signature purple-and-green gradient neon. A large, stylized '50%' is subtly integrated into the texture of the concrete floor, split by the central dividing line. The background is a deep, dark abyss, emphasizing the court as a strategic battleground. The lighting is moody and architectural, with light casting long shadows.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A dramatic, low-angle, vertical shot of a futuristic basketball court. The court is meticulously crafted from highly polished, dark concrete, reflecting the intense lights. A central, glowing neon half-court line precisely splits the surface. On the left side, all court lines emanate a sterile, corporate white glow. Conversely, on the right side, every line, including the three-point arc and key, pulses with a vibrant, Solana-signature purple-and-green gradient neon. A large, stylized '50%' is deeply engraved into the polished, dark concrete floor, its subtle metallic sheen catching the ambient light, and precisely split by the central glowing neon line. The background dissolves into a deep, dark abyss, dramatically emphasizing the court as an abstract, strategic battleground. The lighting is intensely moody and architectural, characterized by dominant neon glows casting vivid colors and deep, well-defined, dramatic shadows that stretch long across the polished surfaces. The composition is a monumental, low-angle view looking upwards, emphasizing epic scale and immense height, with ample negative space at the top to enhance its imposing presence. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, cinematic lighting, ultra-realistic.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic basketball court as the semantic anchor because it directly references the company's name, '6th Man Ventures,' and provides a powerful metaphor for a strategic playing field. The 50% pivot is visualized by splitting the court's aesthetic exactly in half, with one side representing the generic VC space and the other glowing with Solana's brand colors. I included the text '50%' as a large, architectural element integrated into the court's floor to anchor the core statistic of the story diegetically.
visual_idea.description: A macro, top-down shot focusing on the half-court line of a polished concrete basketball court. The image is split in two. One side features a channel of cool white neon light embedded in the dark, textured concrete. The other side shows an identical channel, but glowing with the iconic purple-to-green gradient of the Solana ecosystem. The focus is on the sharp contrast, the texture of the material, and the glowing light reflecting off the polished surface.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, top-down shot focusing on the half-court line of a polished concrete basketball court. The image is split in two by the line. One side features a perfectly straight channel of cool white neon light, seamlessly embedded into the dark, finely textured concrete. The other side mirrors this, showcasing an identical channel, but intensely glowing with the iconic, smooth purple-to-green gradient of the Solana ecosystem. The composition emphasizes the dramatic, sharp contrast between the two light channels and their reflections. Focus is paramount on the intricate texture of the dark, polished concrete, revealing subtle imperfections and microscopic details, including dust speckles, mimicking real-world surfaces. The vibrant, dominant neon glow from both channels casts soft blended neon shadows and interacts dynamically with the highly reflective, polished concrete surface, creating a mesmerizing interplay of light and color that bleeds into the surrounding space. This is a hyper-realistic, architectural study, composed for a 1:1 square aspect ratio, emphasizing perfect symmetry, a strong central focus, and tight macro framing that fills the entire frame with rich texture and luminous detail. 12K resolution, 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, dramatic lighting, studio lighting, volumetric lighting, ambient occlusion, physically based rendering (PBR).

editorial.headline: 6th Man Ventures Pivots 50% of Capital to Solana Apps
editorial.standfirst: Managing Partner Mike Dudas reveals the firm has shifted half its deal flow to the Solana ecosystem, citing data that shows on-chain applications are now outperforming Web2 SaaS in time-to-revenue.
key_takeaways: 6th Man Ventures now allocates approximately 50% of its investment bandwidth specifically to Solana-native founders.
The firm's thesis relies on "Revenue Velocity," noting top protocols hit $100M in annualized revenue faster than traditional startups.
Portfolio winners Pump.fun, Jupiter, and Axiom were cited as primary validation of the "Application Layer" breakout.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The infrastructure phase of the cycle is officially over. During a product keynote at Breakpoint 2025, **6th Man Ventures** Managing Partner Mike Dudas signaled a decisive structural rotation in the firm’s capital allocation strategy. The venture firm, known for early bets on **BONK** and **LinksDAO**, confirmed it now dedicates 50% of its operational bandwidth to the Solana ecosystem. This pivot is not driven by sentiment, but by a hard metric: revenue velocity.

Dudas presented data contrasting the growth trajectories of top crypto protocols against traditional Web2 SaaS unicorns. The analysis revealed that leading Solana applications are reaching $100M in annualized revenue significantly faster than their Silicon Valley counterparts. This "Application Layer" thesis argues that value accrual has moved from speculative governance tokens to cash-flow-positive protocols. **Pump.fun**, **Jupiter**, and the trading terminal **Axiom** were highlighted as prime examples of this trend, where user activity directly correlates to sustainable fee generation rather than inflated exchange metrics.

For institutional investors, this marks a maturing of the asset class. The era of "spray and pray" infrastructure investing is being replaced by a focus on tangible unit economics. **6th Man Ventures** noted that while they remain active in ecosystems like **Base** and **Hyperliquid**, the density of revenue-producing apps on **Solana** makes it the mathematically superior venue for venture returns. The firm plans to continue its bespoke strategy—executing roughly one high-conviction deal per month—targeting pre-seed and seed founders who are building businesses, not just protocols.
